description_long: This object is a vintage-style shotgun with a long, metallic barrel that appears weathered and tarnished, featuring a series of vent holes along the top. The receiver and barrel are constructed from a dark, aged metal, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The grip is made of wood, smoothly curved and polished, with a reddish-brown hue and metallic end cap. The trigger guard is rounded and metallic, with a simple, functional design. The shotgun has a single barrel and lacks any visible pump or lever mechanisms, suggesting a straightforward action. Decorative metalwork is visible near the grip and trigger area, adding to its antique aesthetic.
description: A vintage, single-barrel shotgun with a weathered metal finish and a polished wooden grip, featuring vent holes along the barrel and simple metallic fittings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the vented barrel, wooden grip, and metallic receiver.
description_view_1: Front-on view down the barrel, emphasizing the circular muzzle and the alignment of the barrel with the grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the same features as view 0 but from the other side, with the wooden grip and metallic details visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the grip and the end cap, with the barrel extending away from the viewer.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel with vent holes along the top
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metallic guard with a simple, unadorned design
